---
title: "Pocket Canon"
---

| Sigil | Term                                                                                                                      | Gloss                                                                                              |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0001  | [0001 abstraction](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0001)                                  | Deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about cleanly.                 |
| 0002  | [0002 adapter](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0002)                                      | A translator between unlike shapes that lets systems meet without infecting one another.           |
| 0005  | [0005 anti-corruption layer](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0005)                        | A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.                                     |
| 0006  | [0006 API gateway](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0006)                                  | An edge threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing.                                     |
| 0012  | [0012 boundary](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0012)                                     | The line where one responsibility ends and another begins.                                         |
| 0013  | [0013 bounded context](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0013)                              | A semantic fence: the same term is not forced to mean the same thing everywhere.                   |
| 0023  | [0023 component {Arch}](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0023)                             | A bounded unit of responsibility inside a larger design.                                           |
| 0030  | [0030 coupling](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0030)                                     | The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere.                              |
| 0034  | [0034 dependency inversion](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0034)                         | Stable policy should not kneel before unstable detail.                                             |
| 0036  | [0036 domain model](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0036)                                 | The conceptual map of the business world the software is trying to serve.                          |
| 0043  | [0043 facade](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0043)                                       | A simplified front door hiding a messier interior.                                                 |
| 0055  | [0055 interface {Arch}](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0055)                             | A disciplined seam that lets unlike bodies cooperate without swapping internal organs.             |
| 0058  | [0058 layer](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0058)                                        | A stratum with a narrow job and limited allowed dependencies.                                      |
| 0065  | [0065 middleware](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0065)                                   | Cross-cutting logic placed in the path between request and handler.                                |
| 0066  | [0066 module {Arch}](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0066)                                | A package of related definitions meant to travel as one thing.                                     |
| 0071  | [0071 orchestrator](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0071)                                 | A coordinator that sequences many services or tasks into one larger procedure.                     |
| 0075  | [0075 plugin](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0075)                                       | An extension point that lets capability be added without rewriting the host.                       |
| 0082  | [0082 proxy {Arch}](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0082)                                 | A stand-in that speaks or receives on behalf of another body.                                      |
| 0092  | [0092 service](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0092)                                      | A stable capability exposed for repeated use by others.                                            |
| 0098  | [0098 source of truth](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0098)                              | The place other copies are supposed to agree with.                                                 |
| 0101  | [0101 state machine](../reference/house-architecture-abstraction-and-design.qmd#rune-0101)                                | Behavior expressed as named states and legal transitions.                                          |
| 0120  | [0120 AST](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0120)                                          | The tree form of parsed program structure.                                                         |
| 0124  | [0124 borrow checker](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0124)                               | A checker that enforces safe borrowing and lifetime law before runtime.                            |
| 0132  | [0132 compile-time](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0132)                                 | Known and fixed before execution begins.                                                           |
| 0133  | [0133 compiler](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0133)                                     | The translator from source language to lower-level executable form.                                |
| 0158  | [0158 generic {Lang}](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0158)                               | One definition parameterized over many concrete types or cases.                                    |
| 0159  | [0159 grammar](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0159)                                      | The rules that say what strings count as well-formed structure.                                    |
| 0166  | [0166 inference](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0166)                                    | Recovering missing type or shape information from surrounding evidence.                            |
| 0171  | [0171 interpreter {Lang}](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0171)                           | A runtime that executes a representation rather than precompiling it away.                         |
| 0172  | [0172 IR](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0172)                                           | An internal representation built for analysis and transformation.                                  |
| 0177  | [0177 linker](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0177)                                       | The binder that turns compiled pieces into one loadable whole.                                     |
| 0180  | [0180 macro](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0180)                                        | Code that writes or reshapes code.                                                                 |
| 0188  | [0188 namespace {Lang}](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0188)                             | A naming region that prevents collisions and clarifies ownership.                                  |
| 0197  | [0197 parser](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0197)                                       | The mechanism that turns surface text into structured meaning.                                     |
| 0200  | [0200 polymorphism](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0200)                                 | One interface, many concrete behaviors.                                                            |
| 0218  | [0218 semantics](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0218)                                    | What a program means, not just how it is spelled.                                                  |
| 0229  | [0229 syntax](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0229)                                       | The legal surface form of the language.                                                            |
| 0236  | [0236 type](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0236)                                         | A declared or inferred class of values and allowed operations.                                     |
| 0240  | [0240 typechecker](../reference/house-language-semantics-and-formal-shape.qmd#rune-0240)                                  | The checker that enforces type law before execution.                                               |
| 0252  | [0252 array](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0252)                                              | A contiguous ordered collection accessed by position.                                              |
| 0263  | [0263 buffer](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0263)                                             | A temporary holding area used to smooth movement, parsing, or I/O.                                 |
| 0268  | [0268 checksum](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0268)                                           | A compact fingerprint used to detect accidental change.                                            |
| 0272  | [0272 config](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0272)                                             | Declared settings that shape behavior without editing code.                                        |
| 0280  | [0280 cursor {Data}](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0280)                                      | A movable pointer into a sequence or result set.                                                   |
| 0288  | [0288 dictionary](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0288)                                         | Keyed lookup structure for named values.                                                           |
| 0294  | [0294 entity](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0294)                                             | A thing with stable identity that the system cares about.                                          |
| 0297  | [0297 event](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0297)                                              | A record of something that happened.                                                               |
| 0298  | [0298 event log](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0298)                                          | History stored as events instead of overwritten final state.                                       |
| 0306  | [0306 hash {Data}](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0306)                                        | A condensed fingerprint used for lookup, bucketing, or integrity checks.                           |
| 0310  | [0310 index {Data}](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0310)                                       | An access structure that trades space and write cost for faster reads.                             |
| 0315  | [0315 key {Data}](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0315)                                         | The field by which something is found or grouped.                                                  |
| 0320  | [0320 map {Data}](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0320)                                         | Associative collection from keys to values.                                                        |
| 0322  | [0322 message](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0322)                                            | A discrete packet of meaning sent between parts.                                                   |
| 0338  | [0338 record {Data}](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0338)                                      | A row-like bundle of named fields describing one thing.                                            |
| 0347  | [0347 schema {Data}](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0347)                                      | The declared shape of stored meaning.                                                              |
| 0356  | [0356 snapshot](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0356)                                           | A captured view of state at one moment.                                                            |
| 0359  | [0359 state](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0359)                                              | The currently true condition of a thing that can change over time.                                 |
| 0395  | [0395 decode](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0395)                               | Turn encoded bytes or symbols back into usable meaning.                                            |
| 0402  | [0402 deserialize](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0402)                          | Rebuild structured state from a stored or transmitted form.                                        |
| 0404  | [0404 diff {Verb}](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0404)                          | Make change visible by showing one state against another.                                          |
| 0410  | [0410 encode](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0410)                               | Turn meaning into a transportable or storable representation.                                      |
| 0417  | [0417 extract](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0417)                              | Pull the needed part out of a larger body.                                                         |
| 0419  | [0419 filter](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0419)                               | Keep what matches; discard what does not.                                                          |
| 0424  | [0424 generate](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0424)                             | Produce new structure, code, or data from rules or inputs.                                         |
| 0428  | [0428 hydrate](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0428)                              | Reattach lightweight structure to a previously transferred shell.                                  |
| 0438  | [0438 link](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0438)                                 | Bind separate pieces into one connected whole.                                                     |
| 0440  | [0440 marshal](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0440)                              | Pack structured state into a transportable form.                                                   |
| 0441  | [0441 materialize](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0441)                          | Force a deferred or virtual thing into concrete existence.                                         |
| 0444  | [0444 merge {Verb}](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0444)                         | Combine many lines or branches into one resolved form.                                             |
| 0445  | [0445 migrate](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0445)                              | Move shape, meaning, or state from one regime to another.                                          |
| 0448  | [0448 normalize](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0448)                            | Force many possible surfaces into one agreed shape.                                                |
| 0449  | [0449 optimize](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0449)                             | Trade time, space, or simplicity to improve a measured objective.                                  |
| 0453  | [0453 partition {Verb}](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0453)                     | Turn raw text into structured representation.                                                      |
| 0461  | [0461 quantize](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0461)                             | Change structure to improve clarity while preserving behavior.                                     |
| 0489  | [0489 smooth](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0489)                               | Write structured state into bytes, text, or wire form.                                             |
| 0496  | [0496 synthesize](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0496)                           | Change form, order, or representation while preserving some intended substance.                    |
| 0510  | [0510 verify](../reference/house-transformation-algorithms-and-working-verbs.qmd#rune-0510)                               | Demand evidence that a claim or result actually holds.                                             |
| 0514  | [0514 async](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0514)                               | Work may proceed without immediate blocking at the call site.                                      |
| 0515  | [0515 await](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0515)                               | Pause until an asynchronous result becomes ready.                                                  |
| 0516  | [0516 backoff {Ctrl}](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0516)                      | Wait longer between retries so recovery does not become attack.                                    |
| 0517  | [0517 barrier {Ctrl}](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0517)                      | A synchronization point no participant may cross early.                                            |
| 0527  | [0527 circuit breaker](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0527)                     | Trip open after repeated failure to prevent repeated harm.                                         |
| 0530  | [0530 coroutine](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0530)                           | A suspendable routine that can yield and later resume.                                             |
| 0539  | [0539 enqueue](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0539)                             | Place work onto a queue for later handling.                                                        |
| 0540  | [0540 event loop](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0540)                          | The scheduler that drives nonblocking tasks and callbacks.                                         |
| 0546  | [0546 flow control](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0546)                        | Mechanisms that keep producers from outrunning consumers.                                          |
| 0549  | [0549 future](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0549)                              | A placeholder for a value that will exist later.                                                   |
| 0561  | [0561 mutex](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0561)                               | Mutual exclusion for one critical region at a time.                                                |
| 0571  | [0571 queue {Ctrl}](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0571)                        | Ordered holding area that decouples production from consumption.                                   |
| 0576  | [0576 retry {Ctrl}](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0576)                        | Try again under policy rather than treating one failure as final.                                  |
| 0579  | [0579 schedule {Ctrl}](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0579)                     | Assign work to time, order, or execution slots.                                                    |
| 0581  | [0581 semaphore](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0581)                           | A counted gate controlling concurrent access to limited capacity.                                  |
| 0584  | [0584 signal {Ctrl}](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0584)                       | Emit a condition change another task may respond to.                                               |
| 0598  | [0598 timeout {Ctrl}](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0598)                      | A declared limit on how long uncertainty may persist.                                              |
| 0608  | [0608 yield](../reference/house-control-flow-coordination-and-temporal-logic.qmd#rune-0608)                               | Give up control now so another act may proceed.                                                    |
| 0611  | [0611 allocation](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0611)                                          | Reserve memory for something that is about to live.                                                |
| 0615  | [0615 cache {Run}](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0615)                                         | Keep likely things close so repeated access costs less.                                            |
| 0621  | [0621 copy-on-write {Run}](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0621)                                 | Share until mutation forces a real duplicate.                                                      |
| 0631  | [0631 garbage collection](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0631)                                  | Automatic reclamation of memory no longer reachable.                                               |
| 0634  | [0634 heap](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0634)                                                | Region for dynamically allocated objects.                                                          |
| 0636  | [0636 hot path {Run}](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0636)                                      | The path where small inefficiencies burn real time.                                                |
| 0643  | [0643 lifetime {Run}](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0643)                                      | How long a value may legally remain alive.                                                         |
| 0645  | [0645 locality](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0645)                                            | The performance dividend from keeping related data physically close.                               |
| 0649  | [0649 memory barrier](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0649)                                      | An ordering fence for memory effects across threads or cores.                                      |
| 0652  | [0652 memory safety](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0652)                                       | Protection against illegal memory access and corruption.                                           |
| 0657  | [0657 pinning](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0657)                                             | Keep an object fixed in place so other machinery can safely refer to it.                           |
| 0663  | [0663 reference count](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0663)                                     | Ownership tracked by counting how many references remain.                                          |
| 0670  | [0670 stack {Run}](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0670)                                         | Region for call frames and short-lived local state.                                                |
| 0684  | [0684 zero-copy {Run}](../reference/house-runtime-memory-and-execution.qmd#rune-0684)                                     | Move data without making redundant copies.                                                         |
| 0688  | [0688 cgroup](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0688)                            | A kernel mechanism for bounding resources by group.                                                |
| 0696  | [0696 driver](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0696)                            | Software that speaks directly to a device or kernel surface.                                       |
| 0697  | [0697 epoll](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0697)                             | Linux event notification for many file descriptors at once.                                        |
| 0700  | [0700 file](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0700)                              | A named persistent byte stream in a filesystem.                                                    |
| 0701  | [0701 file descriptor](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0701)                   | A small integer handle standing in for an open kernel object.                                      |
| 0709  | [0709 inode](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0709)                             | Filesystem record describing a file apart from its name.                                           |
| 0714  | [0714 kernel](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0714)                            | The privileged core that arbitrates process, memory, and device access.                            |
| 0720  | [0720 mount](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0720)                             | Attach a filesystem into the visible path tree.                                                    |
| 0729  | [0729 pipe](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0729)                              | A byte stream connecting one process to another.                                                   |
| 0732  | [0732 process {Sys}](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0732)                     | A running program with its own address space and resources.                                        |
| 0755  | [0755 syscall](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0755)                           | The controlled crossing from userland into kernel service.                                         |
| 0760  | [0760 thread](../reference/house-systems-programming-and-operating-system-words.qmd#rune-0760)                            | An independently schedulable line of execution inside a process.                                   |
| 0778  | [0778 backpressure](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0778)                                  | Push resistance upstream when demand outruns capacity.                                             |
| 0791  | [0791 consensus](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0791)                                     | Agreement protocol for shared truth across unreliable nodes.                                       |
| 0799  | [0799 DNS](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0799)                                           | Translate names into network-reachable locations.                                                  |
| 0804  | [0804 endpoint](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0804)                                      | A callable boundary where a service can be reached.                                                |
| 0805  | [0805 eventual consistency](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0805)                          | Agreement postponed rather than denied.                                                            |
| 0806  | [0806 failover](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0806)                                      | Shift responsibility to a surviving node or path after failure.                                    |
| 0815  | [0815 handshake](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0815)                                     | Opening ritual that establishes trust, parameters, or session state.                               |
| 0819  | [0819 HTTP](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0819)                                          | The dominant request-response protocol of the web.                                                 |
| 0822  | [0822 ingress](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0822)                                       | Traffic entering a service boundary.                                                               |
| 0825  | [0825 latency](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0825)                                       | The delay before useful work reaches the caller.                                                   |
| 0828  | [0828 load balancer](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0828)                                 | Distribute incoming work across many backends.                                                     |
| 0834  | [0834 mTLS {Net}](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0834)                                    | Mutual certificate-based identity proof on both sides of a connection.                             |
| 0837  | [0837 network partition](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0837)                             | A split network that makes one cluster behave like several worlds.                                 |
| 0848  | [0848 quorum](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0848)                                        | Enough agreement to act without requiring unanimity.                                               |
| 0852  | [0852 replica](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0852)                                       | A copy kept elsewhere for durability, scale, or locality.                                          |
| 0859  | [0859 route](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0859)                                         | The chosen path by which traffic should travel.                                                    |
| 0862  | [0862 RPC](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0862)                                           | Invoke remote behavior as though it were local, with all the risks that implies.                   |
| 0870  | [0870 socket {Net}](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0870)                                  | A network communication endpoint bound to protocol and address.                                    |
| 0876  | [0876 TCP](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0876)                                           | Ordered reliable stream transport with congestion control.                                         |
| 0884  | [0884 webhook](../reference/house-networking-and-distributed-systems.qmd#rune-0884)                                       | An outbound callback triggered by an event.                                                        |
| 0887  | [0887 ACID](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0887)                                | The classic four-part law of transactional storage: atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability. |
| 0892  | [0892 backup](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0892)                              | A restorable copy taken so loss is not final.                                                      |
| 0898  | [0898 commit {DB}](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0898)                         | Make a proposed change durable history.                                                            |
| 0901  | [0901 constraint {DB}](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0901)                     | A rule the data is not allowed to violate.                                                         |
| 0916  | [0916 foreign key](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0916)                         | A declared reference from one table to another table's key.                                        |
| 0918  | [0918 index {DB}](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0918)                          | An access structure that speeds reads by paying extra write and space cost.                        |
| 0921  | [0921 isolation level](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0921)                     | The degree to which concurrent transactions are allowed to see one another's motion.               |
| 0925  | [0925 materialized view](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0925)                   | A stored query result traded for cheaper future reads.                                             |
| 0926  | [0926 migration](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0926)                           | Change stored reality without losing it.                                                           |
| 0927  | [0927 MVCC](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0927)                                | Versioned rows that let readers and writers coexist more safely.                                   |
| 0933  | [0933 primary key](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0933)                         | The key that uniquely names one row inside its table.                                              |
| 0934  | [0934 query plan](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0934)                          | The strategy the database chose to answer the query.                                               |
| 0943  | [0943 rollback {DB}](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0943)                       | Retreat toward a previously trusted state.                                                         |
| 0948  | [0948 schema {DB}](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0948)                         | The declared shape of database truth.                                                              |
| 0959  | [0959 transaction](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0959)                         | A unit of work meant to stand or fall together.                                                    |
| 0966  | [0966 WAL](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0966)                                 | Write-ahead log: history recorded before dirty pages reach their final home.                       |
| 0974  | [0974 attestation](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-0974)                                     | Evidence that a thing is what it claims to be or was produced the way it claims.                   |
| 0976  | [0976 authenticate](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-0976)                                    | Prove who is making the claim.                                                                     |
| 0978  | [0978 authorization](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-0978)                                   | Decide what an already identified actor may do.                                                    |
| 0990  | [0990 code signing](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-0990)                                    | Cryptographically bind an artifact to a trusted producer.                                          |
| 0998  | [0998 defense in depth](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-0998)                                | Stack multiple protective layers so one failure is not total failure.                              |
| 1002  | [1002 encrypt {Sec}](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1002)                                   | Make plaintext unreadable without the right key.                                                   |
| 1007  | [1007 hash {Sec}](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1007)                                      | Reduce arbitrary input to a fixed fingerprint.                                                     |
| 1012  | [1012 integrity](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1012)                                       | Confidence that data has not been silently altered.                                                |
| 1014  | [1014 key {Sec}](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1014)                                       | The secret or public material that makes cryptographic operations possible.                        |
| 1016  | [1016 key rotation](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1016)                                    | Replace active keys before age or compromise turns them rotten.                                    |
| 1017  | [1017 least privilege](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1017)                                 | Grant only the authority required for the present act.                                             |
| 1028  | [1028 policy](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1028)                                          | A declared rule for how a class of cases is decided.                                               |
| 1037  | [1037 revocation](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1037)                                      | The act of withdrawing trust from credentials or keys.                                             |
| 1042  | [1042 sandbox {Sec}](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1042)                                   | Constrain code inside a smaller, safer prison.                                                     |
| 1044  | [1044 secret {Sec}](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1044)                                    | Information whose power comes from staying hidden.                                                 |
| 1050  | [1050 signature {Sec}](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1050)                                 | Identity or intent made checkable through cryptographic proof.                                     |
| 1054  | [1054 threat model](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1054)                                    | A structured view of likely attackers, assets, and failure paths.                                  |
| 1063  | [1063 zero trust](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1063)                                      | Assume no network location or ambient context deserves automatic trust.                            |
| 1064  | [1064 artifact](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1064)                                    | A produced thing you can store, ship, or compare.                                                  |
| 1068  | [1068 branch {Forge}](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1068)                              | A line of history where change can proceed without touching the main trunk.                        |
| 1069  | [1069 build](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1069)                                       | Turn source into a runnable or shippable artifact.                                                 |
| 1073  | [1073 canary](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1073)                                      | A small production release meant to detect reality before full rollout.                            |
| 1076  | [1076 CI](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1076)                                          | Continuous integration: change is merged only after automated checks speak.                        |
| 1078  | [1078 commit {Forge}](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1078)                              | Record a change as one named point in history.                                                     |
| 1084  | [1084 dependency](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1084)                                  | Something your code needs in order to build or run.                                                |
| 1086  | [1086 deploy](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1086)                                      | Cross the line from forge to world.                                                                |
| 1088  | [1088 diff {Forge}](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1088)                                | A visible account of what changed between states.                                                  |
| 1093  | [1093 feature flag](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1093)                                | A runtime switch that separates deploy from release.                                               |
| 1102  | [1102 lockfile](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1102)                                    | The file that freezes dependency resolution into repeatable history.                               |
| 1104  | [1104 merge {Forge}](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1104)                               | Reconcile divergent lines of history into one.                                                     |
| 1111  | [1111 pipeline {Forge}](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1111)                            | The ordered automation that turns edit into tested artifact.                                       |
| 1117  | [1117 release](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1117)                                     | A named version intended for actual users.                                                         |
| 1120  | [1120 reproducible build](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1120)                          | A build that can be rebuilt into the same bits again.                                              |
| 1123  | [1123 rollback {Forge}](../reference/house-build-tooling-versioning-and-release.qmd#rune-1123)                            | Operational permission to retreat after release.                                                   |
| 1141  | [1141 alert](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1141)                                     | A threshold that wakes humans or automation when evidence crosses concern.                         |
| 1146  | [1146 benchmark](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1146)                                 | Force a performance claim to meet measurement.                                                     |
| 1152  | [1152 contract test](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1152)                             | A promise checked at the seam between systems.                                                     |
| 1163  | [1163 fuzzing](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1163)                                   | Throw weird inputs at the system until hidden assumptions break.                                   |
| 1171  | [1171 integration test](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1171)                          | Test that multiple real parts cooperate correctly together.                                        |
| 1172  | [1172 invariant](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1172)                                 | A law that must remain true while the rest of the system moves.                                    |
| 1174  | [1174 log](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1174)                                       | A recorded stream of events or decisions for later reading.                                        |
| 1176  | [1176 metric](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1176)                                    | A numeric measure that can be tracked over time.                                                   |
| 1180  | [1180 observability](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1180)                             | Enough evidence exists to infer what the system is doing from the outside.                         |
| 1184  | [1184 profiler](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1184)                                  | A measurement tool that reveals where time or allocation is being spent.                           |
| 1186  | [1186 property-based test](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1186)                       | Test the law across many generated cases instead of one remembered example.                        |
| 1187  | [1187 regression test](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1187)                           | A test written so yesterday's bug does not return tomorrow.                                        |
| 1192  | [1192 smoke test](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1192)                                | A fast confidence check that the basic path still breathes.                                        |
| 1202  | [1202 telemetry](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1202)                                 | Measurements emitted in a form other systems can store and alert on.                               |
| 1204  | [1204 trace {Proof}](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1204)                             | A preserved path through execution.                                                                |
| 1207  | [1207 unit test](../reference/house-testing-verification-and-observability.qmd#rune-1207)                                 | Test the smallest meaningful unit in isolation.                                                    |
| 1215  | [1215 atomic instruction](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1215)                  | An indivisible machine operation at instruction level.                                             |
| 1224  | [1224 cache line {HW}](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1224)                     | The coherence-sized chunk of cached memory; too much sharing here hurts.                           |
| 1231  | [1231 DMA](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1231)                                 | Direct memory access: device moves bytes without making the CPU do every step.                     |
| 1235  | [1235 endianness](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1235)                          | The byte order in which multi-byte values are laid out.                                            |
| 1237  | [1237 firmware {HW}](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1237)                       | Software that is closer to the board than the application.                                         |
| 1243  | [1243 GPU](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1243)                                 | A massively parallel processor built for throughput-heavy workloads.                               |
| 1251  | [1251 ISA](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1251)                                 | Instruction set architecture: the machine contract visible to software.                            |
| 1255  | [1255 latency budget](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1255)                      | The maximum delay the system is allowed to spend and still remain acceptable.                      |
| 1269  | [1269 register](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1269)                            | A tiny named storage cell inside the processor.                                                    |
| 1276  | [1276 SIMD](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1276)                                | One instruction operating across many data lanes at once.                                          |
| 1281  | [1281 throughput](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1281)                          | How much useful work passes through in bounded time.                                               |
| 1285  | [1285 utilization](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1285)                         | The fraction of available capacity actually being used.                                            |
| 1287  | [1287 volatile](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1287)                            | A promise to the compiler that this value may change through unusual means.                        |
| 1290  | [1290 zero-copy {HW}](../reference/house-hardware-embedded-and-performance-near-words.qmd#rune-1290)                      | Near-metal data movement without redundant duplication.                                            |
| 1291  | [1291 accessibility](../reference/house-interface-ux-and-human-facing-words.qmd#rune-1291)                                | Make the system usable by people with varied bodies, senses, and tools.                            |
| 1293  | [1293 affordance](../reference/house-interface-ux-and-human-facing-words.qmd#rune-1293)                                   | A cue that suggests what action is possible here.                                                  |
| 1303  | [1303 discoverability](../reference/house-interface-ux-and-human-facing-words.qmd#rune-1303)                              | How easily a user can find what the system can do.                                                 |
| 1306  | [1306 feedback](../reference/house-interface-ux-and-human-facing-words.qmd#rune-1306)                                     | The system speaking back after the user acts.                                                      |
| 1308  | [1308 form validation](../reference/house-interface-ux-and-human-facing-words.qmd#rune-1308)                              | Validation at the form boundary so bad input is caught legibly.                                    |
| 1317  | [1317 keyboard navigation](../reference/house-interface-ux-and-human-facing-words.qmd#rune-1317)                          | Make the interface fully operable without a mouse.                                                 |
| 1331  | [1331 progressive disclosure](../reference/house-interface-ux-and-human-facing-words.qmd#rune-1331)                       | Reveal complexity in stages rather than all at once.                                               |
| 1333  | [1333 responsive design](../reference/house-interface-ux-and-human-facing-words.qmd#rune-1333)                            | Layout that adapts gracefully to device or viewport size.                                          |
| 1346  | [1346 user flow](../reference/house-interface-ux-and-human-facing-words.qmd#rune-1346)                                    | The path a user walks through the product to finish a task.                                        |
| 1350  | [1350 visual hierarchy](../reference/house-interface-ux-and-human-facing-words.qmd#rune-1350)                             | Arrange attention so the right thing is seen first.                                                |
| 1351  | [1351 agent {AI}](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1351)               | A named locus of initiative that may observe, decide, and invoke tools.                            |
| 1358  | [1358 constraint {AI}](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1358)          | A bound that removes degrees of freedom in favor of correctness.                                   |
| 1359  | [1359 context](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1359)                  | The surrounding facts that give the prompt its force.                                              |
| 1364  | [1364 evaluation {AI}](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1364)          | The act of measuring whether the model is actually useful.                                         |
| 1373  | [1373 grounding](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1373)                | Tie generation to evidence instead of to free-association alone.                                   |
| 1374  | [1374 guardrail](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1374)                | A boundary that narrows allowed behavior without fully specifying the answer.                      |
| 1382  | [1382 objective](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1382)                | The outcome the spell is trying to cause.                                                          |
| 1383  | [1383 output contract](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1383)          | The required shape the answer must take.                                                           |
| 1389  | [1389 prompt](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1389)                   | The surface instruction that biases a model toward task, role, and style.                          |
| 1395  | [1395 retrieval](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1395)                | Bring external evidence into the model's working context.                                          |
| 1405  | [1405 spell](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1405)                    | A structured instruction artifact that binds role, goal, context, and checks.                      |
| 1409  | [1409 system prompt](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1409)            | The highest-order framing instruction in the conversation.                                         |
| 1414  | [1414 tool call](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1414)                | A step where the model reaches beyond text into external action.                                   |
| 1419  | [1419 verification {AI}](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1419)        | Truth-checking added to output generation.                                                         |
| 1425  | [1425 atomic](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1425)              | No halfway state is allowed to leak.                                                               |
| 1429  | [1429 available](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1429)           | Reachable and functioning when needed.                                                             |
| 1430  | [1430 backward-compatible](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1430) | New versions do not casually break old clients or data.                                            |
| 1437  | [1437 composable](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1437)          | Small parts combine without excessive friction.                                                    |
| 1440  | [1440 consistent](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1440)          | Different observers are not being told incompatible stories.                                       |
| 1442  | [1442 crash-safe](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1442)          | Failure does not leave storage in nonsense.                                                        |
| 1445  | [1445 deterministic](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1445)       | Same inputs, same result.                                                                          |
| 1446  | [1446 durable](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1446)             | Once acknowledged, the change really sticks.                                                       |
| 1461  | [1461 idempotent](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1461)          | Same request, same lasting effect.                                                                 |
| 1462  | [1462 immutable](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1462)           | Written once, then never changed in place.                                                         |
| 1467  | [1467 linearizable](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1467)        | Behaves as though each operation took effect at one visible instant.                               |
| 1471  | [1471 maintainable](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1471)        | Cheap enough to understand, modify, and keep healthy.                                              |
| 1472  | [1472 memory-safe {Qual}](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1472)  | Will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries.                                           |
| 1474  | [1474 observable](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1474)          | Structured so internal behavior can be inferred from external evidence.                            |
| 1481  | [1481 pure](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1481)                | Same inputs, same output, no hidden side effects.                                                  |
| 1484  | [1484 reliable](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1484)            | Keeps doing the promised thing under ordinary stress.                                              |
| 1485  | [1485 reproducible](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1485)        | Can be rebuilt or rerun and yield the same result again.                                           |
| 1490  | [1490 scalable](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1490)            | Can grow without collapsing the qualities that matter.                                             |
| 1492  | [1492 serializable {Qual}](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1492) | Concurrency behaves as though transactions happened in one clean order.                            |
| 1517  | [1517 cascading failure](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1517)                   | One failure fans out into many dependent failures.                                                 |
| 1520  | [1520 contention](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1520)                          | Too many actors fighting for the same scarce resource.                                             |
| 1521  | [1521 corruption](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1521)                          | Stored or transmitted truth damaged into falsehood.                                                |
| 1525  | [1525 deadlock {Curse}](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1525)                    | Two or more actors wait on one another forever.                                                    |
| 1529  | [1529 dependency hell](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1529)                     | Transitive requirements turning ordinary change into a trap.                                       |
| 1531  | [1531 drift](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1531)                               | Slow departure from the world you thought you were still in.                                       |
| 1534  | [1534 exhaustion](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1534)                          | The resource is simply used up.                                                                    |
| 1537  | [1537 flapping](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1537)                            | Rapid unhealthy oscillation between states.                                                        |
| 1542  | [1542 inconsistent read](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1542)                   | A read that sees a world that never really existed as one coherent moment.                         |
| 1546  | [1546 leak {Curse}](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1546)                        | Something escapes the boundary that should have held it.                                           |
| 1547  | [1547 livelock](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1547)                            | Everyone keeps moving but nobody makes progress.                                                   |
| 1548  | [1548 lost update](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1548)                         | One writer quietly stomps another writer's valid change.                                           |
| 1554  | [1554 outage](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1554)                              | Service unavailable where availability was expected.                                               |
| 1555  | [1555 overflow](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1555)                            | Value exceeds the representable range.                                                             |
| 1558  | [1558 partial failure](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1558)                     | Some of the system failed while the rest kept limping.                                             |
| 1561  | [1561 race](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1561)                                | Outcome depends on timing you do not actually control.                                             |
| 1562  | [1562 regression](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1562)                          | A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.                                              |
| 1564  | [1564 retry storm](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1564)                         | Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it.                                        |
| 1567  | [1567 schema drift](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1567)                        | Stored shape and assumed shape have silently diverged.                                             |
| 1568  | [1568 split-brain {Curse}](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1568)                 | Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.                                             |
| 1584  | [1584 -aware](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1584)                         | Marks that something notices the surrounding condition it names.                                   |
| 1588  | [1588 -driven](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1588)                        | Signals that behavior is organized around events or causes.                                        |
| 1589  | [1589 -first](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1589)                         | Declares priority or design center: security-first, API-first, offline-first.                      |
| 1598  | [1598 -safe](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1598)                          | Asserts a protected behavior such as memory-safe or thread-safe.                                   |
| 1605  | [1605 API-first](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1605)                      | Design the interface surface before the implementations behind it.                                 |
| 1606  | [1606 append-only](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1606)                    | History grows by addition rather than mutation.                                                    |
| 1613  | [1613 crash-only](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1613)                     | Only live briefly, start fast, and recover by restart rather than repair.                          |
| 1616  | [1616 default-deny](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1616)                   | Access is forbidden unless explicitly granted.                                                     |
| 1618  | [1618 event-driven](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1618)                   | Action propagates from events rather than from direct polling or command loops.                    |
| 1620  | [1620 fail-fast](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1620)                      | Abort early when assumptions break instead of smearing damage forward.                             |
| 1623  | [1623 infrastructure-as-code](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1623)         | Operational change expressed as reviewable source.                                                 |
| 1626  | [1626 machine-readable](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1626)               | Structured so software, not only humans, can read it reliably.                                     |
| 1631  | [1631 offline-first {Rune}](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1631)           | Designed to keep working with poor or absent connectivity.                                         |
| 1632  | [1632 policy-as-code](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1632)                 | Policy declared as versioned, testable code.                                                       |
| 1639  | [1639 single source of truth](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1639)         | One canonical place others are expected to mirror.                                                 |
| 1644  | [1644 zero-downtime](../reference/house-compound-forms-prefixes-suffixes-and-naming-runes.qmd#rune-1644)                  | Change introduced without taking the service dark.                                                 |
